conjecture_statement: \text{Let } H_\omega = \Delta + V_\omega \text{ be the Anderson Hamiltonian on } \ell^2(\mathbb{Z}) \text{, where } \Delta \text{ is the discrete Laplacian and } V_\omega \text{ is a random potential defined by a sequence of i.i.d. real random variables } \{v_n(\omega)\}_{n \in \mathbb{Z}} \text{. If the common distribution } \mu \text{ of the } v_n \text{ is non-deterministic (i.e., not a Dirac measure), then for almost every realization } \omega \text{ of the potential, the Lyapunov exponent } \gamma(E) \text{ is strictly positive for every energy } E \in \mathbb{R}.}
lean4_sketch:
import Mathlib.Analysis.InnerProductSpace.Spectrum
import Mathlib.Analysis.NormedSpace.Operator.ContinuousLinearMap
import Mathlib.Probability.Independence.Basic
import Mathlib.Analysis.SpecialFunctions.Exponential

-- We work on the Hilbert space ℓ²(ℤ, ℂ)
-- For the sketch, we define the Hamiltonian and Lyapunov exponent abstractly.
open ContinuousLinearMap ProbabilityTheory MeasureTheory

noncomputable section

-- The Anderson Hamiltonian for a given realization of the potential v
def AndersonHamiltonian (v : ℤ → ℝ) : (ℓ²(ℤ, ℂ)) →L[ℂ] (ℓ²(ℤ, ℂ)) := sorry

-- The Lyapunov exponent, whose definition depends on the transfer matrix formalism.
def lyapunov_exponent (v : ℤ → ℝ) (E : ℝ) : ℝ := sorry

-- This property asserts that an eigenfunction `ψ` for an eigenvalue `λ` decays exponentially.
-- This is a key signature of Anderson localization.
def IsExponentiallyLocalized (ψ : ℓ²(ℤ, ℂ)) : Prop :=
  ∃ (C α : ℝ) (n₀ : ℤ), C > 0 ∧ α > 0 ∧ ∀ n : ℤ, ‖ψ n‖ ≤ C * Real.exp (-α * |(n : ℝ) - n₀|)

-- The theorem states that for any eigenvalue, the corresponding eigenfunction is localized.
-- This is a consequence of, and implies, positivity of the Lyapunov exponent.
theorem anderson_localization_implies_positive_lyapunov
    -- Context: A probability space for the random potential
    {Ω : Type*} [MeasureSpace Ω] (P : Measure Ω) [IsProbabilityMeasure P]
    -- The random potential `v`
    (v : Ω → ℤ → ℝ)
    -- Assume `v` is i.i.d. and non-trivial
    (h_iid : iIndepFun (fun i => (fun ω => v ω i)) P)
    (h_nontrivial : ∃ n, ¬ IsProbabilityMeasure (P.map (fun ω => (Measure.dirac (v ω n)))))
    : ∀ᵐ ω ∂P, ∀ (E : ℝ) (ψ : ℓ²(ℤ, ℂ)),
        (AndersonHamiltonian (v ω)) ψ = (E : ℂ) • ψ → IsExponentiallyLocalized ψ := by
  filter_upwards
  intro ω hω E ψ h_eigen

  let H := AndersonHamiltonian (v ω)

  -- Sub-Lemma 1 — Prove the operator is self-adjoint / bounded
  have h_sa : IsSelfAdjoint H := by
    -- This follows from the fact that the discrete Laplacian is self-adjoint and a re
